quote.text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than he seeks.
quote.author: John Muir

fun_fact.text: Hungarian paprika isn't just one spice—there are eight official grades ranging from delicate and sweet to hot and pungent! The town of Szeged and Kalocsa are so famous for paprika production that they each have museums dedicated to this beloved red spice.

short_read.title: The Secret Lives of Garden Hummingbirds
short_read.teaser: These tiny jewels can remember every flower they've visited and how long it takes each one to refill with nectar. Discover the surprising intelligence behind their iridescent beauty.

medium_read.title: Olympic National Park's Ancient Rainforests
medium_read.teaser: The mossy, misty forests of the Olympic Peninsula are among the quietest places on Earth, and some of their trees have been growing for over 500 years. Learn what makes these temperate rainforests so special and rare.

long_read.title: The Women Who Mapped the Ocean Floor
long_read.teaser: Marie Tharp created the first detailed map of the ocean floor in the 1950s, revealing underwater mountains and the Mid-Atlantic Ridge that proved continents drift. Her story is one of persistence, brilliance, and changing how we see our planet.

port_angeles.title: Dungeness Spit National Wildlife Refuge
port_angeles.description: Just a short drive from Port Angeles, the Dungeness Spit is one of the longest natural sand spits in the world at 5.5 miles long. It's a wonderful place to spot harbor seals, over 250 species of birds, and enjoy stunning views of the Strait of Juan de Fuca and the Olympic Mountains.

puzzle_1.question: A hummingbird's heart beats approximately ___ times per minute while hovering.
puzzle_1.correct_response: That's right, Barbara! An amazing 1,200 beats per minute—about 20 times faster than ours!
puzzle_1.close_response: Very close! A hummingbird's heart actually beats about 1,200 times per minute while hovering—incredibly fast!
puzzle_1.wrong_response: A hummingbird's tiny heart beats about 1,200 times per minute—that's 20 beats every single second!
puzzle_1.learn_more_text: Despite weighing less than a nickel, hummingbirds have the highest metabolism of all animals. Their hearts can slow down to 50 beats per minute when they enter torpor (a hibernation-like state) at night to conserve energy.

puzzle_2.question: Which Hungarian word means 'butterfly'?
puzzle_2.correct_response: Wonderful, Ildiko! Pillangó is indeed butterfly—such a lovely, musical word!
puzzle_2.wrong_response: The Hungarian word for butterfly is 'pillangó'—doesn't it sound graceful? (Virág means flower, madár means bird, and szitakötő means dragonfly.)
puzzle_2.learn_more_text: Hungarian is part of the Finno-Ugric language family, making it quite different from neighboring European languages. Many Hungarian words are beautifully descriptive and poetic.

puzzle_3.question: What do harbor seals, commonly seen in the Strait of Juan de Fuca, use their whiskers for?
options: Detecting fish movements in murky water
puzzle_3.correct_response: Exactly right! Harbor seals have incredibly sensitive whiskers that can detect the tiniest vibrations from fish swimming nearby, even in dark or cloudy water.
puzzle_3.wrong_response: Harbor seals use their sensitive whiskers to detect fish movements in murky water—they can even follow a fish's trail minutes after it has passed by!
puzzle_3.learn_more_text: Each whisker on a harbor seal's face is packed with nerve endings, making them so sensitive they can detect the wake of a fish from several feet away. This helps them hunt successfully even at night or in the often cloudy waters of the Pacific Northwest.

game.title: Bubble Shooter
game.description: A relaxing bubble puzzle game — aim and pop matching colored bubbles. Easy to pick up, fun to play!
